summary: Rare early set with fiery Stealin' and Cream Puff War
blurb: A brief three‑song set showcases the young Dead firing on all cylinders, highlighted by an explosive Stealin' and a punchy Cream Puff War. Jerry's lead work is especially striking, and the vintage soundboard capture preserves the raw energy of this 1966 performance.
review: On October 7, 1966 at the Fillmore Auditorium the Grateful Dead delivered a concise but electrifying set that has become a collector’s gem. The five‑song run—Stealin', Don't Ease Me In, Cold Rain And Snow, Cream Puff War and Walkin' Blues—captures the band in their formative era, with Garcia’s guitar work standing out as “unique and outstanding” on Stealin'. While the exact details of the venue are still debated, the soundboard tape (gd66-10-07.sbd.unknown.14102.sbeok.shnf) offers clear, well‑cut vintage audio that lets listeners hear every nuance of this high‑energy performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The sole recording analysis labels the sentiment as mixed, providing no consistent positive or negative trend to adjust the rating.

best_recording.reason: Clear vintage soundboard capture that preserves the rare three‑song set with excellent fidelity and minimal dropouts.

key_highlights: Rare early three‑song set featuring Stealin' and Cream Puff War
Jerry Garcia's distinctive, standout licks on Stealin'
Good vintage SBD quality for a 1966 recording

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers unique and outstanding licks, especially on Stealin'.
